name: ascon-crypto-ip
description: Ascon-AEAD128 authenticated-encryption core (NIST SP 800-232 lightweight cryptography). Clean-room parameterized SystemVerilog: 128-bit key/nonce/tag, single-round iterative permutation datapath, streaming block interface. Verified against all 1089 official NIST known-answer vectors via C/Python golden models plus Verilator and cocotb differential fuzzing.

interfaces.name: operation_control
interfaces.description: Start pulse and per-operation mode. Inputs are latched on start_i.

signals.name: start_i
signals.description: begin a new AEAD operation (single-cycle pulse)

interfaces.name: key_nonce_tag
interfaces.description: Key, nonce, and expected-tag inputs. Lane order {word1, word0}, little-endian bytes within a 64-bit lane.

interfaces.name: associated_data
interfaces.description: Associated-data rate-block stream (valid/ready). bytes = 16 for a full block, 0..15 for a partial last block.

interfaces.name: message
interfaces.description: Message rate-block stream: plaintext in / ciphertext out for encrypt, ciphertext in / plaintext out for decrypt.

interfaces.name: result
interfaces.description: Completion, computed tag, and decrypt tag-match.

signals.name: tag_ok_o
signals.description: decrypt: 1 if computed tag matched exp_tag_i

rtl_files.description: shared parameters and the Ascon permutation round function
iterative single-round permutation with start/done handshake
Ascon-AEAD128 encrypt/decrypt control FSM and datapath

testbenches.name: ascon_core_cocotb
testbenches.description: NIST known-answer conformance + randomized differential vs Python golden reference + corrupted-tag rejection

testbenches.name: ascon_core_diff_fuzz
testbenches.description: C++ differential fuzzer: RTL vs C golden model across randomized AD/PT lengths (4000 trials)

testbenches.name: golden_model_kat
testbenches.description: C golden model vs all 1089 official NIST SP 800-232 vectors

roadmap: APB / AXI-Lite register wrapper for SoC integration
